title: Analyzing the suitability of LCIA methods to foster the most beneficial food loss and waste prevention action in terms of environmental sustainability
description: A mapping study of European Union projects identifies and evaluates Life Cycle Impact Assessment methods for selecting the most environmentally beneficial food loss and waste prevention actions. It demonstrates the suitability of the Environmental Footprint v3.0 method through comparative LCA scenarios on fresh tomato supply chains, highlighting how midpoint weighting and single-score aggregation can guide decision-making.
